stemHtml: Which statement is correct regarding the regulation of smoking on board of an aircraft?

options.a: Smoking is NEVER allowed on board any aircraft during commercial operations and it is the Commander's responsibility to enforce the rule.
options.b: The Commander may authorise smoking in areas where the smoking does NOT affect the other passengers, such as in the lavatories.
options.c: Smoking is allowed during any phase of flight when authorised by the Commander, with the only exception being if oxygen is used in the cabin.
options.d: The Commander may or may not authorise smoking provided the Operator has a procedure for it and the aircraft is NOT being refuelled or defueled.
